explanation: The property captures the requirement that 'Start-up Mode' is only to be initiated (i.e., activated) when the system is connected to all of the following: breathing circuit (but not to the patient), air supply, and power source, and upon pressing the power button. There is no atomic proposition that explicitly refers to air supply connection, but 'airSupplyConnected' is available. For connection to breathing circuit, 'breathingCircuitConnected' is available, and for the power button being pressed, 'powerButton' is available. The requirement further specifies that the patient must not be connected (so '!patientConnected'), and only when ALL these are satisfied and the power button is pressed, 'StartUpMode' should be initiated. This is a trigger-upon condition, so the correct translation is: 'upon (breathingCircuitConnected & !patientConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & powerButton), StartUpMode'. This structured NL accurately reflects the scenario in the input.
decision2_substring: initiated by pushing the power button of the system once the system has been connected to the breathing circuit (without connection to the patient), the air supply, and the power source
output_structured_natural_language: upon breathingCircuitConnected & !patientConnected & airSupplyConnected & powerConnected & powerButton, immediately satisfy StartUpMode

explanation: The requirement specifies that the Start-up Mode should only be initiated by pressing the power button, and this is only allowed after the system has been connected to the breathing circuit (but not to a patient), the air supply, and the power source. This is a synchronization and enablement requirement: Start-up Mode may only be initiated after all required connections are present and the user presses the power button. Since the atomic propositions do not have explicit propositions for 'connected to power source' or 'connected to air supply', but only 'airSupplyConnected' and 'breathingCircuitConnected' (the latter interpreted as the system and breathing circuit, not patient connection), and no explicit 'power source' connection (we assume from context that system boot assumes power is available if powerButton is pressed), the boolean enabling condition is: breathingCircuitConnected & !patientConnected & airSupplyConnected. The user action to trigger this is 'powerButton', and the result is entering 'StartUpMode'. Thus, the structured template is: 'only after bool_exp1, whenever bool_exp2, immediately satisfy bool_exp3', yielding the logic: only after (connections in place and not patient), whenever (power button pressed), immediately satisfy (StartUpMode).
decision1_substring: once the system has been connected to the breathing circuit (without connection to the patient), the air supply, and the power source
decision2_substring: by pushing the power button of the system
decision3_substring: the Start-up Mode shall be initiated
output_structured_natural_language: only after breathingCircuitConnected & !patientConnected & airSupplyConnected, whenever powerButton, immediately satisfy StartUpMode

explanation: The requirement specifies that Start-up Mode should only be initiated after the power button is pressed and the necessary system connections (breathing circuit, air supply, power source) are made, and the patient is not connected. Mapping this to atomic propositions, StartUpMode represents the mode being initiated, powerButton represents the power button being pressed, powerConnected and airSupplyConnected reflect the essential connections, and patientConnected (negated) ensures patient is NOT connected. The property is expressed as: Start-up Mode initiation (i.e., StartUpMode) happens only upon power button press, and only after (and not before) power, air, and breathing circuit are connected, with the patient NOT connected. Thus, 'after (powerConnected & airSupplyConnected & !patientConnected), upon powerButton, StartUpMode' best reflects the logic, using the atomic propositions provided.
decision1_substring: once the system has been connected to the breathing circuit (without connection to the patient), the air supply, and the power source.
decision2_substring: by pushing the power button of the system
output_structured_natural_language: after powerConnected & airSupplyConnected & !patientConnected, upon powerButton, immediately satisfy StartUpMode

Additionally, the patient should NOT be connected when this is performed. These logical relationships are captured by constructing a boolean expression where all required connections are present, the patient is not connected, and the power button is pressed. The property uses the 'whenever' template to capture that whenever all the prerequisite conditions hold and the power button is pressed, the system should enter Start-up Mode. This faithfully represents the natural language specification.
